comments.author: @reggriffiths5769
comments.text:
This has to be the first time I've heard of Wales being a peninsula, when in fact is the continuation of a landmass in its own right. When you say that a coastline "winds" I believe you mean "Winds" - as in Winding, and pronounced "wynding," ergo "Wynd" Wow, it's even got lighthouses - just like all over the world! Is that one of your "Most amazing places?" Just a few moments of reserach would have told you how to pronounce the place names, but like most other purveyors of travelogues and documentaries, that's too much effort for you. It all boils down to the ignorant preaching to the ignorant!
How do you calculate the age of the rocks at 500 million years, when the Bible (via calculations) deternines the earth being only 10,00 - 12,000 years old? One or the other is clearly wrong, and with the Americans, they rarely get ANYTHING right! 
"Celtic" is the name for a football team, while "Celic" pronounced "Keltic" is what what you should have said as in Kelic Roots/Nation/traditions etc. D'you see what I mean about proper research?
Sorry, there's no such place a Kerr-ner-fon Castle. Do youir bloody research!
Prine Charles was already a Prince from birth; in 1969 he was invested by the Queen (Queen Elizabet II),  with the title Prince of Wales, this being a hereditary title to the firstborn male of the reigning monarch. Again, do youR damned research!
"...in the centuries of centuries." Do you know what you are talking about? Don't you look through you work before publishing? First time seeing the word "arts" pronounced as "crafts!"
The Brecon Beacons is pronounceD with the emphasis on the first syllable, not the second, if indeed you know what a syllable is! Simple research would have told you the correct pronunciation!
Conwy wasn't "just like a Guardian," it WAS a guardian, and the reason it was built! The Llyn Peninsula was more the home of the ancient Druids than Anglesey. The Romans slaughterd the Druids of the peninsula according to history, but you clearly didn't do your research! (funny how that word crops up every time)!
Never seen "small beaks that look like penguins!" Just how bad does this travelogue get? But then we hear we hear of the lighthous reahed by 400 steps ...that descends vertically to the sea - so are we going up or down in this one sentence? Clearly the author/narrator hasn't discovered proper English Grammar construction! He says "vertically," the text reads "vertiginously!" Yes, it did get worse!
These second-rate narrators/documentarists - whatever you want to call them, reslly don't have the first idea of what they're taliking about. They should not be using foreign (to them) languages (including English) if they can't use correct pronunciation. The need to research their subject thoroughly, and need to know English since they do have a (very) basic knowledge that they sort-of use every day. Throughout the  25 minutes I've actually watched of this nonsense, there has been one gross error after another. However, they've been paid by Youtube for their (minimal) effort, and that's all they wanted. I'll mark this channel as one to avoid in future. I couldn't bear to watch any more.
